mcqs.question: In a decision tree, which node represents the starting point of the tree?
mcqs.explanation: The ROOT NODE is the topmost node of the decision tree that represents the best attribute to begin splitting the dataset.

mcqs.question: What does entropy measure in decision tree learning?

options.text: The randomness or impurity of a dataset

mcqs.explanation: Entropy measures the randomness or impurity of a dataset. It is 0 when the dataset is pure (all instances belong to one class) and maximum when the dataset is uniformly distributed across classes.

mcqs.question: Which attribute selection measure is used by the ID3 algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: The ID3 algorithm uses INFORMATION GAIN to select the best attribute for splitting at each node. It chooses the attribute that provides the highest information gain.

mcqs.question: What is the value of entropy when all instances belong to the same class?
mcqs.explanation: When all instances belong to the same class, the dataset is PURE and entropy equals 0.

mcqs.question: Which node in a decision tree assigns a class label?
mcqs.explanation: LEAF NODE is the terminal node that assigns a class label to the instances that reach it without further splitting.

mcqs.question: What problem does overfitting cause in decision trees?
mcqs.explanation: Overfitting causes the tree to learn noise in training data, resulting in POOR GENERALIZATION to unseen data.

mcqs.question: What is the range of Gini Index?
mcqs.explanation: Gini Index ranges from 0 (pure) to 0.5 (maximum impurity for binary classification).

mcqs.question: Which technique involves stopping tree construction early to prevent overfitting?
mcqs.explanation: Pre-pruning (early stopping) stops tree construction early when no statistically significant improvement is found, preventing overfitting.

mcqs.question: What does information gain measure?

options.text: Reduction in entropy after a split

mcqs.explanation: Information Gain measures the REDUCTION IN ENTROPY achieved by splitting the data on a particular attribute.

mcqs.question: Which algorithm uses Gini Index for attribute selection?
mcqs.explanation: CART (Classification and Regression Trees) uses Gini Index for attribute selection in binary classification.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of decision trees?
mcqs.explanation: Decision trees are prone to OVERFITTING, especially when the tree becomes deep and complex, which can be addressed through pruning.

mcqs.question: In decision tree pruning, what is removed from the tree?

options.text: Sections that provide little classification power

mcqs.explanation: Pruning removes SECTIONS THAT PROVIDE LITTLE CLASSIFICATION POWER to reduce complexity and improve generalization.

mcqs.question: What type of node represents a test condition on an attribute?
mcqs.explanation: INTERNAL NODE represents a test condition on an attribute and has one or more child nodes representing different outcomes.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of decision trees?

options.text: Can handle both numerical and categorical data

mcqs.explanation: Decision trees do NOT always produce the optimal tree as they use greedy approach and local decisions.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity for building a decision tree?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity for building a decision tree is O(n log n * d) where n is the number of instances and d is the number of attributes.

mcqs.question: What happens when entropy is maximum (uniform distribution)?
mcqs.explanation: When entropy is maximum (uniform distribution), INFORMATION GAIN IS ZERO because there is no reduction in uncertainty.

mcqs.question: Which of these is a post-pruning technique?
mcqs.explanation: Reduced error pruning is a POST-PRUNING technique that removes sub-trees from a fully grown tree to improve accuracy on validation data.

mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of attribute selection measures?
mcqs.back: To find the most homogeneous subsets

options.text: To find the most homogeneous subsets

mcqs.explanation: Attribute selection measures aim to find the MOST HOMOGENEOUS SUBSETS by selecting attributes that best separate classes.

mcqs.question: Decision trees are suitable for which type of learning?
mcqs.explanation: Decision trees are used for SUPERVISED LEARNING where the training data has known class labels.

mcqs.question: What does a branch in a decision tree represent?
mcqs.explanation: A BRANCH represents the OUTCOME OF A TEST at an internal node, leading to child nodes.
